content.problemStatement:
There are NNN slots in a row, numbered from 111 to NNN from left to right respectively. Each slot is either empty or has a coin in it. It is given that the NthN^{th}Nth slot is **always empty**.

Whenever Chef is standing in ithi^{th}ith slot, where ithi^{th}ith slot is empty, he collects all the consecutive coins to his right, ie. he collects all the coins from (i+1)th(i+1)^{th}(i+1)th to (i+k)th(i+k)^{th}(i+k)th slot, assuming each of these kkk slots has a coin in it and (i+k+1)th(i+k+1)^{th}(i+k+1)th slot is empty.

After this collection, Chef jumps to (i+k+1)th(i+k+1)^{th}(i+k+1)th slot, which is empty, and repeats this collection process until he lands on jthj^{th}jth slot such that, either jjj is NNN or (j+1)th(j+1)^{th}(j+1)th slot is empty. After this, his collection journey ends.

You are given the initial state of all NNN slots. You need to answer 2 types of queries:


111 lll rrr (r<N)(r < N)(r<N) - Swap the state of all lthl^{th}lth to rthr^{th}rth slots, ie. an empty slot gets a coin and a slot with coin becomes empty.
222 xxx - Print amount of coins Chief will collect if he was to start his collection journey from xthx^{th}xth slot. **It is guaranteed that xthx^{th}xth slot is empty**.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ ‚Äî the number of slots and the number of queries to process, respectively.
The next line contains a binary string s of length NNN, such that if sis_isi‚Äã is 000, then ithi^{th}ith slot is empty, and if sis_isi‚Äã is 1, then ithi^{th}ith slot has a coin in it.
The next QQQ lines contain the queries, in each query the first integer represents the type of query, ie. 111 for the first type of query, 222 for the second type of query.

For the query of type 111, the next two integers lll and rrr in the same line represent the range.
For the query of type 222, the next integer xxx in the same line represents the empty slot from where Chief starts his journey.






Output Format
For each query of type 2, output the answer to the query in a new line.
constraints: The sum of NNN and QQQ over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initially, Chef is standing at the first slot, he will take the coin at the second slot and jump to the third slot, and then he will take the remaining two coins and jump to the sixth slot where his journey ends.


After two updates, the slots look like 000101000010100001010, Chef will stay at the first slot and can not collect any coins.
